item.title: Microsoft Offers Voluntary Retirement to About 7% of US Workers
item.description: Microsoft Corp. is offering voluntary retirement to thousands of its employees in the US.

item.title: Shale Bosses See ‘Chaos’ From Iran War in Anonymous Fed Survey
item.description: US shale executives are frustrated with the “chaos” in oil markets that has come from the Middle East conflict, according to the latest energy survey from the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas.

item.title: ECB’s Nagel Says Trump’s Fed Attacks Caused Flight to Safety
item.description: President Donald Trump’s repeated attacks on the Federal Reserve are backfiring as they cause distrust in US institutions and a flight from US assets and the dollar, European Central Bank Governing Council member Joachim Nagel said.

item.title: Too Tipsy to Drive? German Suppliers Have In-Car Sensor for That
item.description: Volkswagen AG, Stellantis NV and Mercedes-Benz Group AG models may soon feature touchscreens able to test blood alcohol levels for drivers.

item.title: CAAT Pension Earns 8.4% as Stock Gains Outweigh Weak PE Returns
item.description: CAAT Pension Plan earned 8.4% last year as buoyant stock performance outweighed soft returns in the fund’s private market portfolio.

item.title: Czech Utility Giant CEZ Plans Asset Split Before Possible Buyout
item.description: Czech power utility CEZ AS outlined a plan to split its assets, a move that could pave the way for the government to obtain full ownership of its electricity production in a long-planned buyout.

item.title: Pimco Privately Lends $10 Billion to Gulf in Wartime Bond Deals
item.description: As Persian Gulf states build cash buffers to deal with any potential economic fallout from the Iran war, one large buyer has stepped in: Pacific Investment Management Co.

item.title: Avis Shares Plunge 62% in Two Days in Crashing Halt to Rally
item.description: A searing surge in the shares of Avis Budget Group Inc. has hit a wall, with the stock plunging over 62% in just two days.

item.title: AI Junk-Bond Binge Brings Rare Early Repayments to Sweeten Deals
item.description: As a more than $20 billion borrowing frenzy to build out data centers descended on the junk-bond market this year, some issuers offered up a rare sweetener: an early cash payback.

item.title: Apollo’s Ingenico Starts Talks With Lenders on ‘Untenable’ Debt
item.description: French payments firm Ingenico, backed by Apollo Global Management, has started debt talks with creditors as it struggles to keep up with its interest bill.

item.title: Banks Kick Off Talks on Lone Star’s €1.5 Billion Lonza Unit Debt
item.description: Banks are preparing to sell €1.5 billion ($1.75 billion) of debt financing backing Lone Star Funds’ acquisition of Lonza Group AG’s capsules and health ingredients business.

item.title: US Business Activity Expands While Prices Rise Most Since 2022
item.description: US business activity picked up in April, fueled by the strongest manufacturing growth in nearly four years as war-related supply disruptions prompted a scramble for supplies.

item.title: Andurand Lost 52% in April First Half as Ceasefire Hurt Oil Bets
item.description: Energy trader Pierre Andurand’s largest hedge fund plunged about 52% in the first half of April, wiping out first quarter gains made on bullish oil bets at the start of the Iran war.

item.title: Eoptolink’s Profit Surges on Brisk Demand for AI Components
item.description: Eoptolink Technology Inc. posted record annual profit and a surge in first-quarter earnings, as demand rises for optical components critical to artificial intelligence development.

item.title: Vail Resorts Sees Earnings at Low End of View on Dearth of Snow
item.description: Vail Resorts, Inc. said its fiscal-year earnings will be at the low end of guidance after poor snowfall led to a slump in total skier visits to its North American resorts this season.

item.title: New Mountain Raises $2.4 Billion to Hold Azuria Water Longer
item.description: New Mountain Capital collected $2.4 billion to extend its hold on infrastructure firm Azuria Water Solutions, underscoring the booming market for secondhand private equity stakes.

item.title: South African Reform Drive Loses Steam as Rail, Ports Lag
item.description: South Africa’s reform drive is losing steam as delays to rail and port overhauls and private-sector participation weigh on momentum.

item.title: Google Faces EU Pressure to Open Up Android to Gemini Rivals
item.description: Google faces ramped up European Union pressure to lift barriers to rival AI search assistants on Android handsets in an escalation that the US giant fears could compromise users’ security and privacy.

item.title: UK Consumer Sentiment Falls to Lowest Since 2023, GfK Says
item.description: Britons became more pessimistic about their finances and the wider economy after the Iran war drove up petrol prices, according to a closely watched survey.

item.title: UK Health Data Listed for Sale on China’s Alibaba After Breach
item.description: Health records from 500,000 people who are participating in British research into aging and diseases were made available for sale on an Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. website.

item.title: Estrangeiros colocam R$ 65 bi na bolsa e locais ficam à margem
item.description: O aumento no fluxo de capital estrangeiro para o mercado de ações brasileiro deve se estender pelo resto do ano, em meio à retomada do apetite por risco nos mercados globais, segundo executivos de grandes bancos.

item.title: Foreigners Tighten Hold on Brazil Stocks as Locals Sit Out Rally
item.description: The surge in foreign inflows into Brazilian equities is set to extend for the rest of the year amid revived risk appetite across global markets, according to top banking executives.

item.title: US Futures Decline as US-Iran Impasse Continues, Oil Price Jumps
item.description: US stock-index futures fell before the bell on Thursday as traders awaited developments in the Middle East and pored over corporate earnings.

item.title: US Jobless Claims Edge Up to 214,000 as Layoffs Stay Limited
item.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits rose last week, though they remain at a level consistent with low layoffs.

item.title: JPMorgan Readies Fresh Private Credit Push After Needling Market
item.description: After years weighing how to dive deeper into private credit, JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s $4.3 trillion asset manager is committing to a strategy that will plow tens of billions of dollars into loans sourced by the firm’s commercial bankers.

item.title: Mexico Inflation Slowed Down in Early April as Iran War Weighs
item.description: Mexico’s annual inflation decelerated slightly less than expected in early April, potentially keeping a second straight interest rate cut at Banxico’s next policy meeting in play.

item.title: Hasbro Preliminary Revenue Beats Estimates, Buoyed by ‘Magic’
item.description: Hasbro Inc. reported preliminary revenue that beat analysts’ estimates, buoyed by its fantasy card game Magic: The Gathering. The toy company said it couldn’t release full quarterly results at this time due to a previously disclosed cybersecurity attack.

item.title: Lyft to Buy Gett’s UK Taxi Business, Marking Its Third International Acquisition
item.description: Lyft Inc. has agreed to buy London black cab app Gett, its third acquisition in the past year, as the company works to expand its presence beyond the US.

item.title: Serra Verde to Boost Output of Scarce Rare Earths Amid US Deal
item.description: Brazil’s Serra Verde Group — the target of a $2.8 billion acquisition by USA Rare Earth Inc. — expects about one-third of its future production to come from heavy rare earth elements, a category currently dominated by China.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for April 23, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.4% as of 7:40 a.m. in New York as US-Iran peace talks remain in limbo.
